t_ch: 但我族的身体只为战争铸造，没有进食的器官。
t_en: But my race is built only for battle. We have no organs with which to feed.

t_ch: 哎哟，还真是……
t_en: Ah, such a pity...

t_ch: 那你是怎么维持生命的？
t_en: Then how do you sustain yourselves?

t_ch: 我等受尼卡多利的金血浇灌，无需外物维生，只以纷争为食。
t_en: We are bathed in the golden blood of Nikador. We need no external objects to sustain ourselves. We feed only on strife.

t_ch: 那你是怎么说话的？
t_en: Then, how do you speak?

t_ch: 我等的言语依靠甲胄间的共鸣，乃是纷争的回响。
t_en: Our language is created using resonance between our helmets and armor, which is the echo of strife.

t_ch: 那你能吹口哨吗？
t_en: Then... can you whistle?

t_ch: …不能。
t_en: ...I cannot.

t_ch: 瞧瞧这天杀的尼卡多利，自己天天喝蜜酿，却不让自己的孩子有嘴品尝美味。
t_en: Get a load of this wretched Nikador. Spending their days drowning in their sacramental wine while never giving their children mouths to sample the flavors of the world.

t_ch: 小帕里斯啊，等你打赢了那个疯王，咱们也把他的嘴封上！
t_en: Young Paris. When you finally defeat the Mad King, let us seal his mouth shut, too!

t_ch: 呵，如此懦弱的男人，面对尼卡多利只会抱头鼠窜吧。
t_en: Hah. A coward like him would run for the hills when faced with Nikador.

t_ch: 是啊，我肯定得跑——要是死在战场上，可就没机会去<color=#dbc291ff>阿卡迪亚</color>了。
t_en: That's right, I'd have to run. If I die on the battlefield, I'll never get my chance to go to <color=#dbc291ff>Arcadia</color>.

t_ch: …阿卡迪亚？
t_en: ...Arcadia?

t_ch: 出现了！阿卡迪亚！
t_en: There it is! Arcadia!

t_ch: 唉，你这孩子怎么还惦记着这事儿，婆婆得再说说你了……
t_en: Tsk, you are still obsessed with that idea? I need to sit you down for a good talking to...

t_ch: 啊！既然咱们的客人不能吃水果，我们就去另外一家店看看吧——
t_en: Ah! Well, since our guest cannot partake of fruits, we will take a look around another store.

t_ch: 你拿自己的祭品当报酬？
t_en: You are using your own offerings as payment?

t_ch: 这就打发我了？
t_en: Are you just getting rid of me like that?

t_ch: 两条命就换点吃的？
t_en: Two lives saved and I get some snacks?

t_ch: 呃…最近手头比较紧，先以此物表达我的心意…我们快走吧，不然她得唠叨到幕匿时了！
t_en: Ughh... Things have been a bit tight lately. Take these as a token of my gratitude for now... We need to hurry off, or she'll nag me till Curtain-Fall Hour!

t_ch: 赞美瑟希斯，愿你令我的子孙理智长存，别写些不三不四的书……
t_en: Praise Cerces! May you grant my descendants wisdom that their writings never grow cheap...

t_ch: 哟，卡拉培，又在祈祷呢？
t_en: Oh, Calpe! Praying again?

t_ch: 帕里斯？你小子没死啊！还好我给你留了新的《金毯密传》——等会儿，你带了个什么过来！
t_en: Paris? You're alive! Thank heavens I saved the new Secret of the Golden Carpet here for you... Just a second, what is that you've brought with you!?

t_ch: 喂，这是咱们奥赫玛的客人，别这么无礼。
t_en: Hey! This is a guest of Okhema. Don't be so rude.

t_ch: 哈抱歉抱歉，我只在战报和故事里听说过纷争的眷属，有点激动了。
t_en: Ho, I apologize. I have only ever read of the Titankin of Strife in battle reports and stories — I got a little startled.

t_ch: 对了，你刚刚提到新的《金毯密传》，这次的内容有提到阿卡迪亚吗？
t_en: So, this Secret of the Golden Carpet you mentioned, does it say anything about Arcadia in it?

t_ch: 又出现了，阿卡迪亚！
t_en: There it is again, Arcadia!

t_ch: 好好听！
t_en: Music to my ears!

t_ch: 这次能来点有效信息吗？
t_en: Will we get any information this time?

t_ch: 这次能不搞断章了吗？
t_en: Please don't leave us hanging this time!

t_ch: 没有，估计是作者开新坑去了。现在大伙更爱看黄金裔英雄跟黑潮怪物对抗的剧情，这种乌托邦题材已经过时啦。
t_en: It does not. I imagine writer have moved on to other interests. Nowadays, people want to read about the heroes of Chrysos Heir doing battle with the monsters of the black tide. This kind of utopian storylines has grown old now.

t_ch: 嘁，没品位！
t_en: Tsk, they have no taste!

t_ch: 等等，我刚刚就想问…阿卡迪亚是什么？
t_en: Wait, I meant to ask... What is this Arcadia?

t_ch: 你们悬锋城人这么不爱看书吗？阿卡迪亚是墨涅塔编织的人间仙境，是它送给瑟希斯的礼物之一。在金丝的缠缚下，天谴之矛无法攻入，灰黯之手无法触及……
t_en: Do you not read in Castrum Kremnos? Arcadia is a wonderland woven by Mnestia. It was one of their gifts to Cerces. Safe under the bindings of the golden thread, the Lance of Fury cannot pierce it and the Hand of Shadow cannot touch it...

t_ch: 呵，听上去是人们恐惧纷争与死亡而编造出的故事罢了。
t_en: Hah. It sounds like no more than a story created by those that fear strife and death.

t_ch: ——不，阿卡迪亚肯定是存在的。因为我手中就有从阿卡迪亚流出的<color=#dbc291ff>蜕生金丝</color>。
t_en: ...No. Arcadia definitely exists. Because I hold in my hand a <color=#dbc291ff>Golden Thread of Rebirth</color> from Arcadia itself.

t_ch: 帕里斯从衣物中拿出了一枚纺锤，上面缠绕着几条金色的丝线，在黎明之下熠熠生辉。
t_en: Paris produces a spindle from his robes, around which a number of golden threads glisten in the dawn light.

t_ch: 哦哟，居然舍得在别人面前掏出你的宝贝了？
t_en: Oh, I never expected you to reveal your treasure to others?

t_ch: 蜕生金丝……
t_en: Golden Thread of Rebirth...

t_ch: 我曾于黑潮之中救下一位逃难的公主，为表感谢，她才将此国宝赠予我。我每日能感受到这丝线在对我低语，呼唤着我前往那遥远的异乡……
t_en: I once rescued a princess as she fled from the black tide. As her thanks, she gifted me this national treasure. Every day I can hear it whispering to me, calling me to go to a distant land...

t_ch: 总有一天，我会找到那个地方，躲开这无休无止的纷争和虎视眈眈的死亡，和所爱之人一起享受永恒的浪漫。
t_en: One day, I will find this place. I will escape this endless strife and ever-looming death and enjoy an eternal romance with the one I love.

t_ch: 呵呵，你的所爱之人是谁呢？不会就是送给你这宝贝的<color=#dbc291ff>俄诺涅女士</color>吧？
t_en: Hah, who is this one you love? Surely it is not <color=#dbc291ff>Lady Oenone</color>, the one that gifted you this treasure?

t_ch: 这人物关系有点复杂。
t_en: This guy really gets around...

t_ch: 感觉要开始胃疼了…
t_en: Am I sensing a love triangle...?

t_ch: 有瓜！
t_en: Ooh, gossip!

t_ch: 别开这种玩笑了，若不是往日的恩情，我连与她对话的资格都没有。
t_en: Don't joke like that. If I had not saved her life, I would not even have the right to speak with her now.

t_ch: 比起受万人爱戴的元老，更合适的旅伴反倒是另一种人——未曾沐浴过爱的人，未曾理解过何为浪漫的人。
t_en: Rather than the elder that already enjoys the love of thousands, I am more suited to another type of traveling companion... One that has never felt love before, one that has never understood the true meaning of romance.

t_ch: 说到这里，帕里斯饶有兴味地看向天谴猎手。
t_en: Having said these words, Paris turns to the Furiae Archer with obvious admiration in his eyes.

t_ch: 无论她是敌人还是异类，我都希望自己能成为浪漫的眷属，亲自将那美好的金丝缠在那位旅伴的身上，绞断她原本冰冷的命运。
t_en: Be she an enemy or an outsider, I hope that I could become her romantic companion. That I could wind that perfect golden thread around her body and finally break the cold fate she once faced.

t_ch: …真是无趣而肤浅的话题，在这里继续讨论你的妄想吧，我还是对那边店中的盔甲更感兴趣。
t_en: ...Such a pointless and shallow topic. Continue indulging in your delusions as you please. I have more interest in the armor in that store over there.

t_ch: 等等，那不是盔甲，是裙子啊——
t_en: Wait, that's not armor. Those are dresses...

t_ch: 唉，这姑娘…恩人，麻烦你看着她点，别被戍卫当成敌人给敲碎了。
t_en: Ah, that girl... My savior, could you keep an eye on her? Please do not allow the guards to mistake her for an enemy and reduce her to rubble.

t_ch: 帕里斯，你这套话术逗逗城里的姑娘就算了，对那种东西也能说出口啊？
t_en: Paris. I get you teasing the city girls with that sweet talk, but you'd really say those things to something like that?

t_ch: 你们瑟希斯的传人真是保守，在「浪漫」面前，万物都应一视同仁……
t_en: You descendants of Cerces are far too narrow-minded. In the eyes of "romance," all should be seen as equal.

t_ch: 她在看什么？
t_en: What is she looking at?

t_ch: 不会在这儿还要收集这个吧？
t_en: She's not still collecting those things here, is she?

t_ch: 嘘，这附近好像有熟悉的味道……
t_en: Shh, there is a familiar scent nearby.

t_ch: 奥赫玛的城中…有好多这样的蝴蝶。
t_en: The city of Okhema... There are so many butterflies here.

t_ch: 那是若虫，和你一样，是泰坦的眷属。
t_en: They are nymphs. They are Titankin, like yourself.

t_ch: 是以前的阿格莱雅！似乎和现在没什么差别呢？
t_en: That's Aglaea from the past! She seems no different to how she is today?

t_ch: 因为她是黄金裔吧。
t_en: Probably because she's a Chrysos Heir.

t_ch: 因为她有墨涅塔的力量吧。
t_en: Probably because of Mnestia's power.

t_ch: 回去得问问她怎么保养的。
t_en: We'll have to ask her for her skincare routine.

t_ch: 真是稀奇呀，天谴之矛的士兵在云石市集可不常见——欢迎来到永恒之城，悬锋的客人。
t_en: How unusual! A soldier of the Lance of Fury browsing the Marmoreal Market is a rare sight indeed... Welcome to the Eternal City, guest from Kremnos.

t_ch: …谢谢。
t_en: ...Thank you.

t_ch: 方才你提到这蝴蝶也是泰坦的眷属，是哪一尊泰坦呢？
t_en: You said that these butterflies are Titankin. Which Titan are they descended from?

t_ch: 既然提到蝴蝶，那便是墨涅塔了。
t_en: Well, when it comes to butterflies, they are naturally descendants of Mnestia.

t_ch: 据说，墨涅塔追求瑟希斯的爱而不得，最终抱憾而终。心生垂怜的塞纳托斯拾起那些为爱而逝的亡魂，投入浪漫的残茧，令其孕育出金蝶——也就是这些眷属。
t_en: Legend has it that Mnestia longed for the love of Cerces, but their love was unrequited. They died with a sense of deep regret. Feeling great pity, Thanatos cast those that had lost their lives to love into a cocoon of romance and allowed them to be reborn as golden butterflies... These were the resulting Titankin.

t_ch: 它们如今也在为自己神明所爱的神明忙碌，追寻浪漫的足迹，为墨涅塔收集着疗愈瑟希斯的爱意……
t_en: They now work hard for the love of their beloved god. They trace the trail of romance and collect love to heal Cerces for Mnestia's sake...

t_ch: 呵，这儿的人都在赞颂墨涅塔的痴情，原来只是个一厢情愿的愚神。
t_en: Hah. The people here praise Mnestia for their unconditional love. Turns out they are no more than a foolish deity caught in a one-sided infatuation.

t_ch: 因为用「理性」目光来看，「浪漫」确实是一种无法理解的存在吧。
t_en: That is because if you look at "romance" through the eyes of "reason," it is an existence that one cannot explain.

t_ch: 要我说，让自己沉浸在幻想中，还将幻想附加在其他人的身上，自说自话地上演一个人的悲剧——如此自私的「浪漫」，只是一场一个人的「纷争」。
t_en: If you ask me, immersing oneself in a fantasy and forcing that onto another person is no more than acting out a self-produced tragedy... Such a selfish "romance" is no more than "strife" that one suffers alone.

t_ch: …或许你是对的，许多带来浪漫的东西，实际上都平凡甚至粗劣，但身陷在其中的人很难意识到这点，他们会将泥潭看作甘泉，将粗丝看作金线。
t_en: ...Perhaps you are right. Many of the things that bring romance to our lives are in fact so unremarkable they are almost crude. However, this is something that those who have fallen in love struggle to realize for themselves. They see a swamp as a sweet spring and rough twine as a golden thread.

t_ch: 但是浪漫的幼虫又寄生在每个人的心中，不知何时就会蜕化成蝶，让原本看得清的人花了眼。这是墨涅塔赐予每个人的祝福，可能也是诅咒。
t_en: But, like parasites, the larvae of romance dwell in the hearts of all. We know not when they will metamorphose into butterflies and change our perception from clarity to bedazzlement. This is the blessing that Mnestia grants to all, though it may also be a curse.

t_ch: 姑娘，离愁时快到了，走吧！
t_en: It's almost the Parting Hour, girl! Come on!

t_ch: 是你的朋友在呼唤你吧？告辞，愿金丝不会绊住你的脚步。
t_en: Is that your friend calling you? Farewell. I pray that the golden thread will not hinder your footsteps.

t_ch: 怎么样，我们圣城的生活是不是比天天打架的悬锋城幸福多了？
t_en: What do you think? Isn't life in the holy city much happier than the life of constant fighting of Castrum Kremnos?

t_ch: 是啊，但我为这样的幸福感到可悲。城外的纷争即将攻破城墙，你们的城民却还沉浸在虚假的平和中，恬不知耻地享受安逸。
t_en: It is. But I feel pity for this happiness that you feel. The strife of the outside world prepares to break through your city walls, yet your citizens are immersed in a false sense of peace. You enjoy this tranquility without any shame at all.

t_ch: 相比天谴之矛驯养的喋血狮群，你们不过是刻法勒的溺爱下的一圈羔羊。
t_en: Compared to the bloodthirsty lions bred by the Lance of Fury, you are no more than a flock of sheep coddled by the love of Kephale.

t_ch: 但享受安逸的权利恰恰是奥赫玛能凝聚人心的原因。而你的狮群已经把你抛弃了，不如就披上羊皮，享受一下羊圈里的幸福吧。
t_en: But it is this right to enjoy tranquility that brings the people of Okhema together. Your pride of lions has already abandoned you. Why not wrap yourself in the wool of a sheep and enjoy the happiness of a flock for yourself?

t_ch: 不说话就当你默许了吧。恩人，那就先告辞了，还得去给她找张大地民尺寸的床。
t_en: I'll take your silence as tacit consent. My savior, I will bid you farewell. I must go and find a bed that can sleep a Titankin.

t_ch: 等等…
t_en: Hold on...

t_ch: 我还有一个问题…
t_en: I have a question for you...

t_ch: 嗯？恩人但说无妨，我有问必答。
t_en: Oh? Please, tell me, savior. I will tell you all that I know.

t_ch: 阿卡迪亚到底在哪里？
t_en: Just where on earth is Arcadia?

t_ch: 哈哈，我当然不知道了。如果我有答案，就绝不会在这奥赫玛多逗留一秒。
t_en: Haha, how would I know? If I knew that, I wouldn't linger one second longer in Okhema.

t_ch: 我至今为止的人生，只不过是为了那个还未开始的旅途做准备：参军攒钱，收集情报，还有…寻找愿与我同行的旅伴。
t_en: My entire life till this moment has been no more than making preparations for a journey I've yet to begin. I joined the army to earn money, I have gathered information, next... I must find someone willing to travel by my side.

t_ch: 等我做好出行的准备，一定会将此次恩情的报偿补还给您的！相信我！
t_en: Once my preparations are complete, I will make sure I repay you for this kindness! Believe me!

t_ch: 到现在也不知道阿卡迪亚到底在哪里…帕里斯未来真的去到那个地方了吗？
t_en: We still don't know where Arcadia is... Did Paris really make it there in the future?

t_ch: 记忆的味道似乎越来越奇怪了，我们还得再去更接近现在的往世看看……
t_en: The memory's scent is growing more and more unusual... We will need to go into the more recent past to see...

t_ch: 或许是没有战事让我感受时间的流逝，奥赫玛的岁月飞逝的速度如同飞矢。
t_en: Perhaps the lack of battle meant I also felt the flow of time less acutely, but my days in Okhema have flown by like an arrow.

t_ch: 城中的人们接纳了我，掳走我的士兵愿伴我左右。他们不断向我投来的某种不可名状的事物，那是我在故乡从未感受到的…热情。
t_en: The people of the city have accepted me, and the soldiers that once abducted me became my companions. They constantly affronted me with something that I could not properly describe. It was something that I had never felt in my hometown, a type of... kindness.

t_ch: 但在这情感面前，我却觉得这幅石身中愈发空虚，仿佛热情的温度正在蒸发我纷争的金血。
t_en: However, in the face of these emotions, I felt my stone body becoming more and more hollow... As if the warmth of this kindness was evaporating my golden blood of strife away.

t_ch: 为战而生的偶像逃离了战争，当命运偏离了航道，船上的灵魂也难免迷惘——
t_en: The idol born for war had fled the battlefield, their fate deviates from course. When the ship's trajectory is no longer followed, its passengers' souls become lost in remorse...

t_ch: 于沙场厮杀时，我无需恳请瑟希斯的垂枝，无需驱赶墨涅塔的飞虫。沙与血能遮蔽烦忧，号角声会引我向前。
t_en: When I fight on the battlefield, I need not beg for the willow sprig of Cerces, nor drive away the flying insects of Mnestia. My worries find solace in the blood and sand, and the call of the horn guides me forward.

t_ch: 但在这圣城之中，云石天宫的泉水涤去了沙与血，黎明云崖上的歌声盖过了号角声。站在无云遮蔽的天空下，我反倒是什么也看不清了。
t_en: But in this holy city, the spring water of Marmoreal Palace has washed clean all the blood and sand. The song of Dawncloud silences the horns. Standing under a clear sky, I instead find that I can see nothing at all.

t_ch: 姑娘——你在哪呢？
t_en: Girl... Where are you?

t_ch: 终于找到你了。欢喜月之初的天宫庆典多么热闹，为何独自遁逃，躲在此处？
t_en: I finally found you. The palace's Month of Joy celebration is so lively, but why are you hiding here alone?

t_ch: 我从未参加过此类仪式，不晓得你们的礼节，待在席上怕是会扰了你的兴致。
t_en: I have never attended a ritual like this and do not understand your customs. I fear that my presence would ruin your enjoyment.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，满溢之杯在上！法吉娜的典礼中，最需要注意的礼节便是不要讲礼节！
t_en: Hahaha, oh great Chalice of Plenty! The most important thing during the festival of Phagousa is to not observe any etiquette!

t_ch: 来吧，就算你没有品尝蜜酿的喉舌，也能与我一起聆听乐曲与欢笑！
t_en: Come! Even if you don't have the tongue to savor honey mead with me, you can listen to the songs of celebration and laughter with me!

t_ch: …好吧，那就由你这放肆之人，带我体会一下这放纵的快乐吧。
t_en: ...Very well. Then I shall allow an unbridled man such as yourself to show me the joy of indulgence.

t_ch: 若是你为我带来的喧闹能将空虚填满，我也不厌沉入无底的杯中。
t_en: If the noise that you bring me can fill this void, I would not resist sinking into the bottomless cup.

t_ch: 啊，无姓无名的造物。
t_en: Ah, nameless creation.

t_ch: 你曾举着那天谴的弓矢步步轻移，将箭尖射入敌人的胸膛。
t_en: You once raised high your bow of fury and marched on step-by-step, launching arrows deep into your enemies' chests.

t_ch: 那真是一种光荣的职务，可是如今全白费了。
t_en: Oh, the glory you gained, all of it now in vain.

t_ch: 好！帕里斯，跳得真好！
t_en: That's it! You're such a great dancer, Paris!

t_ch: 我跳得再好，也是大家见惯了的舞蹈。但这场上有一位稀客，说不定能给大家带来不一样的舞蹈——
t_en: No matter how great I dance, it is one that you have all seen before. However, we have a rare guest here today that may be able to show us a new dance like no other...

t_ch: 姑娘，来，跟我共舞一曲吧！
t_en: Miss, come, dance with me!

t_ch: 哎呀，这没什么难的，跟着歌曲舞动身体就行了。
t_en: Come on, it's not difficult! Just move your body to the rhythm.

t_ch: 别害羞啊！给我们看看悬锋城的舞蹈！
t_en: Don't be shy! Let us see the dance of Castrum Kremnos!

t_ch: 喂喂，不会是还放不下以前的心里的坎，不想给奥赫玛面子吧？
t_en: Hey now! You're not still so attached to your past that you cannot do Okhema the honor of a dance, are you?

t_ch: 唉…好吧，我试一试。
t_en: Ah... Fine, I'll try.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，这不是跳得不错嘛！
t_en: Hahaha, not bad at all!

t_ch: 这样…就算是舞蹈了吗？
t_en: You... call that dancing?

t_ch: 等等，姑娘，小心——
t_en: Wait, girl, look out—

t_ch: 砰！
t_en: Crash!

t_ch: 哎呀，这可糟了……
t_en: Oh no, that's not good...

t_ch: 抱歉…我不是故意的。
t_en: I'm sorry... I didn't mean to...

t_ch: 在石像破碎的巨响过后，气氛瞬间沉寂。欢笑退潮之后，泛起了低声私语的泡沫——
t_en: The sound of the statue shattering to the ground brought the entire atmosphere to dead silence. As the laughter faded, the bubbling of whispers began...

t_ch: 这可是用吉奥里亚的圣石雕刻的，被她一下就碰碎了……
t_en: That was carved from the sacred rock of Georios. She smashed it like it was nothing...

t_ch: 是啊，我刚刚还站在她身边…若是撞到我身上，那是不是就直接去见塞纳托斯了？
t_en: That's right. I was just standing next to her... If she had fallen on me, I'd have been sent straight to Thanatos, would I not?

t_ch: 听说这家伙造出来就是用来打仗的，说不定还杀过我们的同胞，为什么要让这种东西来宴会啊……
t_en: I hear this thing was created only for war. It might have even killed our kin! Why should we allow something like that to come to our celebration...

t_ch: 这群人的脸变得真快…
t_en: These people changed their tune quickly...

t_ch: 好一个自适应包容性。
t_en: Such fickle inclusivity...

t_ch: 宇宙评论区现状了属于是。
t_en: Essentially the comment sections of the universe.

t_ch: 喂，你们说什么呢——
t_en: Hey, what are you saying...

t_ch: 诸位，请不要当面侮辱我们的客人。
t_en: Everyone, do not insult our guest!

t_ch: 这位眷属…女士是我们奥赫玛的贵客，不要将偏见带到这包容的城邦。
t_en: This Titankin... This lady is a guest of Okhema. Do not bring your prejudices into this city of acceptance.

t_ch: 戍卫长，泰坦眷属终归和我们凡人之躯有别。让她在这里放开手脚，岂不是在威胁城民的安全？
t_en: Captain, the bodies of Titankin and mortals are not the same. Is allowing her to frolic here freely not endangering the lives of the citizens?

t_ch: 嗯，这话也没错——听到了吗？帕里斯，你不该带她来这里。
t_en: Hm, you have a point... Did you hear that, Paris? You should not have brought her here.

t_ch: 好吧，我们走就是了，这云石天宫又不是没有其他地方可以玩……
t_en: Alright, we'll go. It's not like there is nowhere else in Marmoreal Palace for us to enjoy ourselves.

t_ch: 等等，既然她已经是我们的一员，那也应该遵守我们的法律。
t_en: Hold on. She is one of us now. She must obey our laws.

t_ch: 云石天宫是法吉娜赐予我们的馈赠，也是奥赫玛宝贵的古老财产。依照律法，戍卫队应该将破坏此处的犯人扣押，交由法庭判处。
t_en: The Marmoreal Palace is a gift to us from Phagousa, and the precious property of Okhema. According to law, the Garrison must detain any criminal that damages this place and take them to the court for sentencing.

t_ch: 啊？不用这么较真吧，别忘了她可是俄诺涅……
t_en: Huh? Surely it's not that serious? Don't forget, she is a guest of Elder Oenone...

t_ch: 作为元老的客人，她就可以跳离塔兰顿的制衡吗？
t_en: Are you saying that, as a guest of the elder, she is above the checks and balances of Talanton?

t_ch: 是啊，要是这些外来者可以随意绕开我们圣城的律法，那还像话吗？
t_en: He's right! You think an outsider being above the laws of our holy city is justifiable?

t_ch: 没错，审判她！连同过去伤害我们奥赫玛人的罪责一起审判！
t_en: That's right! Take her for sentencing! Judge her for this damage to Okhema alongside the past offenses she has committed against our people!

t_ch: 看到民意所向了吗？不要再以私情干预此处的公正了，除非你能将这破坏恢复原状！
t_en: Do you see the will of the people? Do not use your personal affairs to interfere with the justice of this land any longer! That is, unless you can restore this damaged item to its previous glory?

t_ch: 或许我能修好。
t_en: I may be able to fix it.

t_ch: 又要球棒侠出手了。
t_en: Galactic Baseballer to the rescue, once more!

t_ch: 我用无敌的泰坦之力想想办法。
t_en: I will use the unmatched power of the Titans to find a way.

t_ch: 对哦，你可以用「欧洛尼斯祷言」来修好这个东西吧！
t_en: You're right! We can fix it with Oronyx's Prayer!

t_ch: 恩人？好久不见，原来你也在这宴会上！
t_en: My savior? It's been a while. I had no idea you were at the celebration, too!

t_ch: 嗯？陌生人，别趁着醉意在这里说胡话了，你要如何让这碎片重新聚合？
t_en: Hm? Stranger, do not allow your drunken revelry to speak nonsense for you. How would you possibly restore these fragments?

t_ch: 这位可是能在悬锋城中来去自如、执掌欧洛尼斯神力的英雄，绝不可能骗你的！恩人，快给他看看你的本事吧——
t_en: This hero can enter Castrum Kremnos as they please and wield the divine power of Oronyx. They would never deceive you! My savior, quickly, show them your talents...

t_ch: 看吧，完好如初！咱就不要太计较了，让客人见了笑话可不好。
t_en: See? Good as new! Let us fight no more. We would not want to cause a scene in front of our guest.

t_ch: 欧洛尼斯的力量…好吧，既然有能人出手相助，我就不多计较了。
t_en: The power of Oronyx... Very well, as a capable hero has stepped in to help, I will take this matter no further.

t_ch: 但是为了大家的安全，还是请这位客人离开这里吧——比起宴会，你更该在战场上起舞。
t_en: However, I would ask you still to leave this place for the safety of our citizens... You are more suited to dancing on the battlefield than at a banquet.

t_ch: 戍卫长，你怎么能这么说！
t_en: Captain, how could you say such things!?

t_ch: 不，他说的没错。
t_en: No, he is right.

t_ch: 身为纷争的使徒，你们过往的敌人，确实不该出现在此处打扰你们的欢宴，告辞。
t_en: As an envoy of Strife and your former enemy, I should not be here to disturb your celebrations. Farewell.

t_ch: 姑娘，等等——
t_en: Miss, wait...

t_ch: 帕里斯，你先给我等等！
t_en: Paris, you wait right there.

t_ch: 自从这个眷属来到了奥赫玛，你就变得愈发散漫。别忘了，你还是圣城戍卫的一员，总有一日要为了保护奥赫玛，而与她的族人相杀。
t_en: Ever since that Titankin came to Okhema, you have grown even more undisciplined. Do not forget that you are also a member of the holy city's garrison. There will come a day when you will have to fight to the death against her kind to protect Okhema.

t_ch: 我很好奇，现在的你在战场之上，还是否能将剑刃劈向相同的面庞？
t_en: I cannot help but wonder... If you were on the battlefield now, could you swing your blade at something with her face?

t_ch: 这是什么意思？难道帕里斯对那个眷属……
t_en: What is he saying? Surely Paris and that Titankin aren't...

t_ch: 我早就听说这小子玩得花，原来还有这种癖好，嘿嘿嘿……
t_en: I had always heard that guy was a playboy. So, he's into that stuff too, hehehe...

t_ch: …我不知道，因为我从来没有勇气直面过那些身为敌人的眷属。
t_en: ...I do not know. To this day, I have never had the courage to face Titankin enemies.

t_ch: 但我知道，刚刚的那位眷属已不是我们的敌人，你不该用言语的剑刃去伤害她。
t_en: But what I do know is that THIS Titankin is no longer our enemy. You should not use your words as weapons against her.

t_ch: 唉，黄金之茧啊，不要再用丝线蒙住我同胞的眼睛了…这样的盲人，怎么面对即将到来的纷争呢？
t_en: *sigh* Oh, Chrysalis of Gold, weave your silk over the eyes of my fellow no longer... How can someone as blind as him hope to face the approaching strife?

t_ch: 元老与战士心中最重要的东西不是城邦的安危，而是无用的情愫……
t_en: The elders and the soldiers no longer hold the safety of this city's people most dear. They prioritize their useless sentiments...

t_ch: 若是人人如此，我们该如何抵御那即将到来的胁迫，难道要乞求墨涅塔用丝线来抵御刀枪吗？
t_en: If we all continue this way, how could we hope to fight against the impending threat? Are we supposed to beg Mnestia and their threads to protect us from their weapons?

t_ch: 恩人，你看到眷属姑娘往何处去了吗？
t_en: Savior. Did you see where the Titankin girl went?

t_ch: 没看到。
t_en: I didn't.

t_ch: 应该到外面去了。
t_en: I think she went outside.

t_ch: 场景就这么大，总能找到。
t_en: There's only so far she can go in this scene.

t_ch: 说的也是，她应该也没走出云石天宫。
t_en: I guess you're right. I doubt she left Marmoreal Palace.

t_ch: 刚刚戍卫长说出那样的话，她应该心里很不好受吧……
t_en: I'm sure the captain's words just now really hurt her...

t_ch: 恩人，也请你帮我找找吧，我估计她就在集市附近。
t_en: Savior, please help me find her. I imagine she must be near the market.

t_ch: 天谴猎手一个人遥望着远方的刻法勒，那石铸的面庞上看不出任何情绪。
t_en: The Furiae Archer sits alone, staring at Kephale in the distance. Their carved stone face shows no emotions at all.

t_ch: …有什么事吗？
t_en: ...Can I help you?

t_ch: 你还好吗？
t_en: Are you okay?

t_ch: 帕里斯在找你。
t_en: Paris is looking for you.

t_ch: 呵呵，他还真是难缠。
t_en: Hah, he just doesn't give up.

t_ch: 别哭，坏人会笑。
t_en: Don't cry. Your enemies will laugh.

t_ch: 我为什么要哭…而且我也没有制造泪水的能力。
t_en: Why would I cry...? I'm not even capable of producing tears.

t_ch: 我的确在想那个戍卫长的话，但并不是感到伤心，而是在思考他说的是不是对的。
t_en: I am indeed thinking about what that captain said, but I am not sad. I am thinking about whether what he said is true.

t_ch: 人类，我和你们不一样。刻法勒赋予了你们这个种族无穷的可能性，你们的身体可以用于任何工作，你们可以信仰任何一位泰坦，可以去做任何事情。
t_en: Humans are different to us. Kephale has bestowed you with infinite possibilities. Your bodies can be used for any kind of work, you can worship any Titan you choose, and you can do whatever it is you please.

t_ch: 而我们眷属不同。我的身体是为纷争铸造的，无法品尝食物，无法与人共舞，无法繁衍后代。若不是瑟希斯的权能流溢于世，我甚至不该拥有思考和言语的资格。
t_en: But we Titankin are not the same. My body is created for strife. I cannot taste food, dance with man, or create future generations. If it were not for Cerces' power overflowing into the world, I would not even have the right to thought or language.

t_ch: 我或许真的该回到战场，这样我才能知道自己是谁——一个连名字都没有的战士，这是我的命运。
t_en: Perhaps I should return to the battlefield. That is the only way that I can truly know who I am... A warrior that does not even have a name. That is my fate.

t_ch: 姑娘，你怎么还再念叨着什么命运？若是命运的枷锁如此牢靠，你早就是悬锋城里的砂砾了。
t_en: Girl, how is it that you are still speaking of fate? If the chains of fate were truly unbreakable, you would have long since been reduced to gravel in Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 帕里斯……
t_en: Paris...

t_ch: 命运有什么好害怕的，那抬秤的泰坦糊涂，拉帘的泰坦盲目，开路的泰坦自己也迷路，执掌命运的就是这么三个迷糊虫而已！
t_en: What is there to fear about fate? The Titan that bears the scales is themself confused. The Titan that pulls the curtain is blind, and the Titan that guides the way is lost. They are no more than three blind mice that guide the strings of fate!

t_ch: 呵呵，在战场上怯懦如鼠，却有胆量咒骂命运的神明，你可真是把勇气用错了地方。
t_en: Hah. You cower on the battlefield, yet wield the courage to curse the gods of fate. I believe this courage of yours is misplaced.

t_ch: 神明不会亲手把剑插进我的胸膛，但你们悬锋城的战士会啊。而且他们尚且无心帮助虔诚祈福的信徒，又怎么会有空来管一个胡言乱语的凡人呢？
t_en: The gods would never pierce my heart with their blade personally, unlike the warriors of Castrum Kremnos. What's more, they cannot find it in themselves to assist their most devoted disciples. Do you think they have time to address a rambling mortal like me?

t_ch: …说的也是，在金宝座上的泰坦眼中，我们不过是微不足道的蝼蚁。
t_en: ...This is true. To the Titans on their golden thrones, we are no more than insignificant ants.

t_ch: 所以来跟我一起来骂吧，向傲慢的命运宣泄不满吧！
t_en: So, why not curse them with me? Come and vent your dissatisfaction with the arrogance of fate!

t_ch: 雅努斯的机关好没意思！
t_en: Janus' mechanisms are boring!

t_ch: 塔兰顿的天秤解密太无聊了！
t_en: Talanton's scale puzzle is boring!

t_ch: 欧洛尼斯送的小狗不够可爱！
t_en: The puppy Oronyx gave me wasn't cute enough!

t_ch: 人家哪里不可爱…不对，才不是小狗！
t_en: How am I not cute enough... Wait, I'm not a dog!

t_ch: 虽然不知道是什么意思，但是咱们的恩人已经做好了示范，该你了！
t_en: I'm not entirely sure what that means but... Our savior has led by example. It's your turn!

t_ch: 假如那三位泰坦真的能听到我的声音，比起咒骂，我更想让它们对我心中的疑虑做出回答……
t_en: If the three Titans could hear me, I would rather they answer the doubts in my heart than curse their existence...

t_ch: 但它们不会回答我的问题，只会在尘世外低吟似是而非的预言，像他们执掌的宿命一样冷漠。如此不负责任的存在，真是一群…一群……
t_en: But they will not answer my questions. All they would do is whisper paradoxical prophecies into the ether. They are as cold as the very fate that they control. Beings that flaunt their responsibilities like that are no more than a bunch of... A bunch of...

t_ch: {RUBY_B#泰坦詈语}Hinas{RUBY_E#}！
t_en: {RUBY_B#Titan curses}Hinas{RUBY_E#}!

t_ch: 呃，什么丝儿？
t_en: Uhhh... What nas?

t_ch: 这…这是泰坦们的语言，它们应该会听得懂。
t_en: That... That was the language of the Titans. I'm sure they understand.

t_ch: 专门骂泰坦的脏话？太酷了，教教我！
t_en: A specific slur against Titans? That's so cool! Teach me!

t_ch: 不，凡人的口舌无法发出我们的言语……
t_en: No, a mortal tongue cannot pronounce our language...

t_ch: 嘿呀，不要小看人类的学习能力——海辣丝儿！
t_en: Hey now, don't underestimate a human's ability to learn... Halnals!

t_ch: …呵呵，真是蠢货。
t_en: ...Hah. You dummy.

t_ch: 雕像小姐的心情看起来好多了，真为她开心！
t_en: The statue lady seems much happier now. I'm happy for her!

t_ch: 但是似乎我们还是没有找到什么与阿卡迪亚有关的信息…得让追忆的岁月继续向前一些……
t_en: But it looks like we still haven't found enough information on Arcadia... We need to take these memories forward a little more...

t_ch: 听，快听。
t_en: Listen, listen now!

t_ch: 听，快听，远方传来了你熟悉的声音。
t_en: Listen, listen now! It comes from afar. You know this sound!

t_ch: 是纷争，纷争已经来到，相撞的刀枪在讴吟，被切开的骨肉在歌啸。
t_en: It is strife. It has come. The clashing of blades sounds like singing, a chorus of flesh cleaved from bone.

t_ch: 而伴随纷争的，是死亡。我听说无数的将士死在圣城前，那血流似溪水，几度要使冥河示现。
t_en: And with strife, comes death. I hear that countless warriors have died before the holy city. Their blood flows like a stream, almost summoning the River of Souls to Earth.

t_ch: 战争的孩子，为何对此熟视无睹？快快迈入这沙场，无论投身哪一边——
t_en: Child of war, why do you turn a blind eye? Join in the battle. It matters not for what side...

t_ch: 不，我已放下了我的弓箭，现在我是奥赫玛城中一位普通的妇人了。
t_en: No, I hung up my bow long ago. I am now an ordinary woman of Okhema.

t_ch: 这些年来，帕里斯教会了我作为人类的生活有多么安逸幸福。我随那大工匠学了锻造。我能为自己敲造银饰，能为帕里斯铸炼佩剑。
t_en: Over the years, Paris taught me the tranquility and happiness to be found in human life. I learned forging under the Grand Craftsman. I crafted silver jewelry for myself and a sword for Paris.

t_ch: 但你最想铸造的是屠戮的武器，是夺命的弓矢，就连给帕里斯的剑都开了饮血的樋。
t_en: But what you wish to forge the most is a murderous weapon, a bow and arrows that can take away life. You even put in a fuller in the sword you give to Paris so it can kill better.

t_ch: …我在卡拉培先生的书上学了炊事。我能做出迷倒人类的食物，能为帕里斯烹饪美味的佳肴。
t_en: ...I learned to cook from Mister Calpe's books. I can create dishes that humans love and prepare delicious recipes for my Paris.

t_ch: 但你最享受的是屠宰牲畜时的快意，你最喜欢的味道是那生肉散发的血腥味。
t_en: But you come alive the most in the moments when you're slaughtering livestock, and your favorite scent is that of raw blood on the chopping block.

t_ch: 我…我还随梅塔涅拉婆婆学了绣花，我为自己编制衣裙，为帕里斯编制挂毯……
t_en: I... I also learned how to embroider from Granny Metanella. I wove dresses for myself and tapestries for Paris...

t_ch: 那挂毯上绣的是奄奄一息的奥赫玛人，和挥舞着青铜武器的悬锋城人——因为你而在尼卡多利手中遭受的无数血战，被你织进了一根根纬线之中。
t_en: The tapestry shows the people of Okhema breathing their last, with those from Castrum Kremnos wielding the bronze weapons... The countless bloody battles that Okhema suffered under Nikador's rule, all because of you, have been woven to life in the tapestries forevermore.

t_ch: 你想战斗。
t_en: You want to fight.

t_ch: 你想杀戮。
t_en: You want to kill.

t_ch: 你想引弦发箭穿头盔铠甲破碎头颅绽放嫣红花蕾前去纷争践踏脆弱躯体成泥玷污白石砖斩骨切肉脆响悦耳前去纷争恐惧憎恨哭嚎环绕燃烧的尸山燃烧残缺败者肢体献祭饮血前去纷争
t_en: You want to draw your arrows and shatter helmets and armor making them bloom red flowers as heads shatter go on to the strife and trample the weak bodies into mud that stains the white marble brick beneath the beautiful sounds of chopping flesh and cutting bones go toward the strife fear hate crying out mountains of bodies burning all around you entrails and limbs of the defeated and the sacrificed go to the strife

t_ch: 我…我想……
t_en: I... I want...

t_ch: 纷争的子嗣，你想上阵作战吗？
t_en: Child of strife, do you wish you go to battle?

t_ch: <size=32>不！我不想！</size>
t_en: <size=32>No! I don't want to fight!</size>

t_ch: 哼，本以为你已成为了奥赫玛的子民，结果不能对你寄予期望。
t_en: Hmph. And I believed that you had become a citizen of Okhema. I knew it was wrong to place any hope in you.

t_ch: 奥赫玛的戍卫长……
t_en: Captain of Okhema...

t_ch: 此刻已是圣城存亡之刻，萨白尼、阿狄安、奥利弗兰卡…奥赫玛的藩邦一个个化为焦土，而天谴之矛已将矛尖指向城门。
t_en: The moment of reckoning has come for the holy city. Sabany, Adian, Alflanke... The vassal states of Okhema have been reduced to scorched earth one by one. Now, the Lance of Fury points its tip at the city gates.

t_ch: 为战而生的存在不愿为这庇护你的城邦而战，是不忍于往日的血亲厮杀、不敢面对自己造主的威能、还是不舍在这城中的某个人？
t_en: A being born for war refuses to fight to protect this city. Could it be that you can't bear to fight with your past relatives? Perhaps you don't dare face the might of your creator? Or maybe there is someone in this city that you cannot leave?

t_ch: 但我要告诉你，你所留恋的那个人马上就会登上战场了。
t_en: Whatever the reason, I must tell you: That man that you cannot bear to part with is about to step onto the battlefield.

t_ch: 什么？
t_en: What?

t_ch: 军令已经下达，身为一个强壮的青年，他有义务捍卫圣城，乃至献出生命——这是他的命运，值得他引以为傲。
t_en: The military orders have been released. As a young man that is fit to fight, he has a duty to protect the sacred city, even if it costs his life. This is his fate, and he should be proud of it.

t_ch: 听，这城门前正放着悲声：有一些妇女在这里呼唤她们的丈夫，有一些在呼唤她们的年老的母亲，还有一些在呼唤她们的儿女，像飞鸟在悲唤雏儿。
t_en: Listen! Hear the sound of wailing from the front of the city gate: Wives call for their husbands and their elderly mothers, while others scream for their children. Just hear how they suffer.

t_ch: 而你，你如今依然在刻法勒的宝座前显露着你石刻的颜面，那没有表情的颜面像是带着笑意。
t_en: And you, you bow before the throne of Kephale with your stone face. Your emotionless expression has almost a smile in its place.

t_ch: 登上战场…若是和帕里斯一起…或许……
t_en: Go to the battlefield... If I am with Paris... Maybe...

t_ch: 唉，墨涅塔啊，请赐予我浪漫的奇迹吧！
t_en: Oh Mnestia, please grant me a romantic miracle!

t_ch: 你怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong?

t_ch: 是不是又要我帮忙了？
t_en: Do you need my help again?

t_ch: 啊，大恩人，真是太久没见了！你总是在我最需要帮助的时候出现，让我不得不相信命运的眷顾了！
t_en: Ah, my savior! It has been far too long! You always appear when I need you most. I cannot help but feel that fate is always watching out for me!

t_ch: 你也知道，现在奥赫玛的战况严峻，悬锋城已经打到城门口了，如今又要动员我们城中的戍卫出战。
t_en: I'm sure you are aware that Okhema is in grave danger. As the forces of Castrum Kremnos have made it to the city gate, now we have to mobilize the guards of the city garrison to battle.

t_ch: 这次敌军带着尼卡多利的神启袭来，上阵的士兵怕是凶多吉少——但我可不想死！
t_en: The enemy forces are attacking with the Revelation of Nikador. I fear any soldier that face them will meet a grim fate... But I don't want to die!

t_ch: 这是你的义务。
t_en: This is your duty.

t_ch: 真是个草包。
t_en: You are truly useless.

t_ch: 又想当逃兵了？
t_en: You want to desert your duty again?

t_ch: 恩人，我不是不爱这养育我的圣城，但还有理想没有完成——我还没有见到阿卡迪亚，我不想在步入冥河的时候都没能见到它一面！
t_en: My savior, it is not that I do not love this city that raised me. It's just that I have a dream that I have not yet completed... I have still not seen Arcadia. I don't want to enter the River of Souls without ever having laid eyes on it!

t_ch: 所以，随他们怎么说吧，叛徒、逃兵，懦夫…我都无所谓，义务、责任、使命我都可以背弃，但我一定要到那个地方。
t_en: So, let them say what they will about me. Traitor, deserter, coward... My duty, my responsibility, and my mission, I will happily abandon them all, but I must reach that place.

t_ch: 我已备好了出逃的行囊，但在这之前，我希望能有人与我同行。你知道是谁的，对吧？
t_en: I have already prepared my bags for my escape. But, before I go, there is someone that I hope would accompany me. You know who that is, right?

t_ch: 是那个泰坦眷属吧。
t_en: The Titankin, right?

t_ch: 你俩进展这么快？
t_en: Aren't you two moving a bit fast?

t_ch: 呵呵，朝夕相伴这么久了，而且明眼人都能看出我的心意。
t_en: Hah, we have spent many days and nights together now. Anyone with sense can see how I feel for her.

t_ch: 是我？这不好吧？
t_en: Me? But wouldn't that be wrong?

t_ch: 哈哈，恩人，你就别在这种场合跟我开玩笑了。
t_en: Haha, my savior, this is not the time to be joking with me.

t_ch: 我希望能与她一同离开，哪怕我们死在路上，也能用浪漫的翅膀逃离塞纳托斯的手掌，化作一对相伴的飞蝶飞向那个地方。
t_en: I want to leave with her. Even if we die on our path, we can fly into the hands of Thanatos together on the wings of romance. We can become butterflies and fly to that place together.

t_ch: 恩人，这份恋情离不开你的帮助。在我心中，你一直是墨涅塔化身一般的存在。
t_en: Savior, this love of mine has always relied on your assistance. In my heart, you are like an embodiment of Mnestia herself.

t_ch: 现在，我要向她表明我的心意与愿望。请你为我见证，为我祝福，给我力量…这是我最后拜托你的一个忙了。
t_en: Now, I will confess my love and wishes to her. Please bear witness and give me your blessings and strength... This is the last favor I will ask of you.

t_ch: 祝福你，加油！
t_en: You have my blessings. Go for it!

t_ch: 你俩过好日子比什么都强。
t_en: Nothing is more important than your happiness.

t_ch: 我就在旁边吃个瓜。
t_en: I'll be here with my popcorn.

t_ch: 谢谢恩人，那我就去了，愿你能再次给予我好运。
t_en: Thank you, savior. I will go now. I pray that you can bring me good fortune once more.

t_ch: 帕里斯，我听说你要奔赴战场了，是吗？
t_en: Paris, I hear you are planning to go to the battlefield. Is that true?

t_ch: 啊，原来你已经知道了……
t_en: Ah, so you already knew...

t_ch: 是的，而我也已经做出了决定。
t_en: I did. And I have already made my decision.

t_ch: 太好了，那我们就——
t_en: Wonderful, then let's —

t_ch: 一起逃跑吧！/一起并肩作战吧！
t_en: Run away together!/Fight together, side by side!

t_ch: 什么……
t_en: What...

t_ch: 并肩作战？不不不，开什么玩笑，我会死在战场上的！
t_en: Fight side by side? No, no, no... Are you joking? I'll die on the battlefield!

t_ch: 可我会保护你的，我愿背弃悬锋城与尼卡多利，将一切力量为奥赫玛，为你所用！
t_en: But I will protect you. I will abandon Castrum Kremnos and Nikador, and give all of my power to Okhema. Use it as you please!

t_ch: 我会成为你最好的战友，与你共同承担这伟大的义务与使命，一起守卫……
t_en: I will become your greatest ally and share the weight of your duty and mission. Let us guard...

t_ch: 别说了，姑娘！
t_en: That's enough, girl!

t_ch: 什么义务，什么使命，我根本不在乎，让悬锋城和奥赫玛带着它们一起见冥河去吧！
t_en: Duty? Mission? I don't care about those things! Castrum Kremnos and Okhema can disappear into the River of Souls with them for all I care!

t_ch: 姑娘，这个城中我在乎的只有你一人，其他人都无所谓…你愿意跟我走吗？
t_en: Girl, you are the only person that I care about in this entire city. No one else matters... Will you go with me?

t_ch: 不要再犹豫了，我们去找阿卡迪亚，躲开纷争与死亡，永远在浪漫中生活下去！
t_en: Hesitate no more. Let us go in search of Arcadia! We can escape strife and death and live a life of eternal romance!

t_ch: 你可以继续锻造饰品、烹饪美味的食物、编织美丽的挂毯，让你在奥赫玛找到的新生活在那里延续……
t_en: You can continue crafting jewelry, cooking delicious meals, and weaving beautiful tapestries. Your new life that you discovered in Okhema... you can continue it there...

t_ch: 而我们彼此也不应该是什么战友，而应该是更重要、更浪漫的存在。
t_en: We were never meant to be allies in battle. Our relationship is something more important, something more romantic.

t_ch: …好啊，那…我们…一起走吧。
t_en: ...Alright. Then... let's... let's go.

t_ch: 太好了！既然这样，我在奥赫玛城中的理想也算是实现了！
t_en: Felicitations! My Okhema dream has finally come true!

t_ch: 帕里斯掏出了自己怀中的纺锤，将丝线的一段缠绕在自己的戒指上。
t_en: Paris pulled out the spindle from his chest pocket and wrapped a section of the thread around the ring on his finger.

t_ch: 他犹豫了片刻，继而将自己最为珍视的这捆金丝递给了面前的泰坦眷属——
t_en: He hesitated for a second, then proceeded to hand his most precious golden thread to the Titankin before him...

t_ch: 来，姑娘，拿好，它的长度足以延展到城外了。
t_en: Come, girl, hold this. It is long enough to stretch outside the city.

t_ch: 我们一起行动太过惹眼，你先混着攻势出城。半个时段之后，我循着丝线的方向来找你。
t_en: It is too obvious if we make our move together. You use the chaos of the attack to slip out of the city first. In half an hour, I will follow the golden thread to find you.

t_ch: 呵呵，循着金丝找到命定之人的位置，像是墨涅塔的寓言一样，是不是很浪漫？
t_en: Hah, following the golden thread to locate the person you are fated for... it's just like the fable of Mnestia. Don't you think it's romantic?

t_ch: …嗯，是的。
t_en: ...Yes, it is.

t_ch: 是吧！你就快趁着幕匿时先动身吧，现在大部分人都在睡觉，我马上就来找你！
t_en: Isn't it? Leave quickly while the hour of Curtain Fall is still with us and most people are still sleeping. I will come and find you soon!

t_ch: 浪漫…还真是难懂啊……
t_en: Romance... I will never understand it...

t_ch: 太好了，恩人，多亏了有你的见证才这么顺利！
t_en: Oh felicitations, my savior! This all went so smoothly thanks to you bearing witness.

t_ch: 不客气。
t_en: Not at all.

t_ch: 其实和我没啥关系。
t_en: This actually had nothing to do with me.

t_ch: 我怎么觉得气氛不太对…
t_en: Why did that seem a little off to me...?

t_ch: 哈哈，她一直是个容易害羞的姑娘，你又不是不知道。
t_en: Haha, she's always been the shy type. You know this.

t_ch: 恩人，你也不想在这里跟尼卡多利拼个你死我活吧？不如和我们一起走，路上还能有个照应。
t_en: My savior, you don't wish to fight to the death with Nikador here either, do you? Why not come with us, so that we can look after each other along the way?

t_ch: 接下来应该就要去找阿卡迪亚了吧？我们就跟他一起走吧！
t_en: They're going to Arcadia now, right? Let's go with him!

t_ch: 姑娘，这是怎么回事？
t_en: Girl, what is the meaning of this?
Umm...

t_ch: 帕、帕里斯，快帮帮我们！
t_en: Pa—Paris! Help us!

t_ch: 不会吧，难道是你把戍卫长打成这样的？
t_en: It can't be... Did you do this to captain?

t_ch: 帕里斯，你在说什么！？这个泰坦眷属是——
t_en: Paris, what are you saying!? This Titankin is...

t_ch: 小心！
t_en: Look out!

t_ch: 她是入侵者！
t_en: She's an intruder!

t_ch: 上当了！
t_en: It's a trick!

t_ch: 呼——还好有恩人在，但为什么悬锋城的战士会闯进城里？
t_en: Phew... Thank the heavens you are here, savior. But how did the soldiers of Castrum Kremnos get into the city?

t_ch: 当然…是因为…因为你！
t_en: Isn't it obvious...? This is all... your fault!

t_ch: 因为我？
t_en: My fault?

t_ch: 是你让那邪物踏足了奥赫玛…令城中的戍卫对眷属放松了警惕…而她如今就在城外攻打奥赫玛的队伍中——这就是你救下的邪物，恩将仇报的叛徒！
t_en: You allowed that wicked creature to step foot inside Okhema... You caused the city guards to lose their vigilance around Titankin... Now, she has joined the team outside the gates to attack Okhema... This is the creature that you saved. See how your kindness is repaid with vengeance and betrayal!

t_ch: 姑娘她，怎么会…我们明明约好了……
t_en: That girl, she... How could... We made a promise...

t_ch: 戍卫长！我们事后再来定罪，你伤势太重，请不要再多言了！
t_en: Captain! We can settle blame later. Your wounds are severe. Please save your words.

t_ch: 咳！我…我必须要说…在塞纳托斯降临前，我要诅咒你们这两个混账……
t_en: Ack! I... I must say this... Before Thanatos descends... I curse you two vile scoundrels...

t_ch: 你们的那些什么浪漫…爱…是我们用牺牲和鲜血来维护的…而我还要舍命保护你们这些盲目的愚者……
t_en: You and your romance... Your love... It is us that have sustained it with our blood and sacrifice... And now I have to pay with my life to protect you blind fools...

t_ch: 我诅咒你们…得不到{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}的指引…得不到{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的启示…被{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}予以严正的审判……
t_en: I curse you... to never gain {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}'s guidance... to never gain {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}'s guidance... to face stern judgement at the hands of {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#}...

t_ch: 永远…见不到…那该死的墨涅塔……咳！
t_en: May you never... lay eyes on... your godforsaken Mnestia... Ahhck!

t_ch: 戍卫长？戍卫长！
t_en: Captain? Captain!?

t_ch: 元老，他已经…被灰黯之手牵走了。
t_en: Elder, he... He has been taken by the Hand of Shadow.

t_ch: 这、这是因为我们的错吗？是因为我们将纷争的眷属放了进来，所以才会变成这样！
t_en: Was... Was this our fault? Is this only happening because we allowed the Titankin of strife to enter the city!?

t_ch: 你冷静一点…
t_en: Calm down...

t_ch: 这不是你们的错…
t_en: This is not your fault...

t_ch: 确实。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 果然，我果然不配做一位元老……
t_en: I knew it... I was never worthy to serve as an elder...

t_ch: 不、不，不是的！这都是命运的罪！
t_en: No, no, that's not true! This is all fate's fault!

t_ch: 是命运让我们将善意与怜悯赋予了错误的人！是命运让恶果降临在了戍卫长身上！
t_en: It is fate that caused us to show kindness and mercy to the wrong person! It was fate that brought this dire ending on the captain!

t_ch: 唉，但我们怎么能审判命运呢——那执掌审判的泰坦也在判决命运的王座上啊。
t_en: Ah, but how are we supposed to cast judgment on fate... The Titan that presides over judgment also sits atop its throne.

t_ch: 俄诺涅元老，抱歉…我无法参与此事的审判，我必须要走了，城外还有人在等我。
t_en: Elder Oenone, I am sorry... I cannot take part in this judgment. I must go. Someone is waiting for me outside the city.

t_ch: 你…你要叛逃？和那个正在攻打我们的泰坦眷属一起逃走？
t_en: You... You are going to desert us? You are going to flee with that Titankin that is attacking our city as we speak?

t_ch: 帕里斯，不要再被那颗带来灾厄的祸星蛊惑了！我已经让你做了错事，绝不能再让你错下去！
t_en: Paris. You must not allow yourself to be bewitched by this bringer of calamity any longer! I have already allowed you to make one mistake. I cannot allow you to continue!

t_ch: 可是……
t_en: But...

t_ch: 你若与她为伍，则会永远背负着共犯的罪责，永远成为被奥赫玛人怨恨的恶徒。
t_en: If you side with her, you will forever bear her sins. You will become a villain, hated by the people of Okhema for eternity!

t_ch: 可是……
t_en: But...

t_ch: 而、而且，戍卫们会将菲罗克的死怪罪于你们！或许还会将你们捉回奥赫玛，处以最严厉的审判！
t_en: Further... Furthermore, the garrison guards will all place the death of Philoc on both of your heads! They may even bring you back to Okhema and see that you receive the most brutal of judgment!

t_ch: 可是…可是除了她，还有谁愿意帮我抛下这可怕的责任与义务，相信我所说的故事，和我一起去寻找阿卡迪亚呢？
t_en: But... But if I don't have her, who else will help me escape my terrifying duties and responsibilities? Who will believe my stories and go in search of Arcadia with me?

t_ch: 帕里斯，我的英雄，你还有我。为何要将金丝赠予一个丑陋的邪物，而不是将它归还它的主人呢？
t_en: Paris, my hero. I am here for you. Why would you give the golden thread to a vile, evil creature like that and not return it to its original owner?

t_ch: 我的权力可以让你远离战场，远离这些纷争的噩梦。我们一起躲在黎明云崖上，在刻法勒的膝下安然度日。
t_en: With my influence, you can be spared from the battlefield and kept far away from the nightmares of strife. Let us escape to Dawncloud and spend our days in peace under the protection of Kephale.

t_ch: 然后等到那黑潮落下，纷争败退，我们以尊贵的身份开着装饰花朵的马车去寻找的浪漫之乡——
t_en: Then, when the black tide falls and strife has been defeated, we can ride in our grand carriage as nobles and go in search of our city of romance...

t_ch: 阿卡…迪亚。
t_en: Arca... dia.

t_ch: 是的，阿卡迪亚。以金丝为证，那是我的故国见证过的地方，我能帮你找到它……
t_en: That's right, Arcadia. With the golden thread as my witness, it is a land known to the people of my hometown. I can help you find it...

t_ch: 这是在搞什么？
t_en: What are you playing at?

t_ch: 这只是给我所爱的人一个新的选择，有错吗？
t_en: I'm just giving the person I love another option. Is there something wrong with that?

t_ch: 你可真会找机会挖墙脚。
t_en: A home wrecker knows when to strike.

t_ch: 你怎能这样污蔑我…这金丝的主人本就是我，我比那个怪物更适合陪伴他吧？
t_en: How dare you slander me like that? ...I am the original owner of the golden thread. Am I not far more suitable to accompany him than that monster?

t_ch: 至少去找找雕像小姐吧！
t_en: At least go and look for the statue lady!

t_ch: 你是说让帕里斯跑到城外的战场上，冒着生命危险在一群疯狂的眷属中找她吗？
t_en: You're saying that Paris should run out onto the battlefield and risk his life among those insane Titankin to look for her?

t_ch: 恩人…我、我觉得俄诺涅说的没错。
t_en: Savior, I... I think Oenone is right.

t_ch: 对，没错！菲罗克不会骗我，那姑娘…天谴猎手一定是被她的同类唤起了纷争的本能，选择回到了悬锋城的军队中，背叛了我！
t_en: Exactly! Philoc would not lie to me. That girl... The Furiae Archer's battle instincts must have been awakened by her kin. She must have chosen to return to the forces of Castrum Kremnos... She has betrayed me...

t_ch: 啊！我不能背负叛逃的罪责！俄诺涅，我的公主，请你帮帮我吧！
t_en: Ah! I cannot bear the blame of betrayal! Oenone. My Lady! Come and aid me!

t_ch: 放心吧，帕里斯。我会让你远离那个灾星，帮你去往你理想中的地方，忘掉那骗子吧，好吗？
t_en: Fear not, Paris. I will keep you away from that bringer of catastrophe and help you find the land of your dreams. Forget that liar, okay?

t_ch: 在城外的厮杀声中，在戍卫长尚未瞑目的尸首旁，两人相拥，像是躲入了看不见的茧里。
t_en: Surrounded by the sounds of the fighting outside the city, the two embrace as if wrapped in an unseeable cocoon. They stand beside the corpse of the captain, his eyes open past the precipice of death.

t_ch: 他们的面庞上的笑容是那么牵强，眼神中的惶恐胜过快乐。即便在两张俊美的脸上，也显得有些诡异。
t_en: The smiles on their faces are forced as fear devours any joy in their eyes. Though their faces are beautiful, there is something distorted about this image.

t_ch: 伙伴…这里的记忆开始变淡了，后面是雕像小姐不知晓的事情，我们已经没法再探寻了……
t_en: Partner... The memory here is starting to fade. The statue lady doesn't know what happens after this, so we cannot investigate any further...

t_ch: 但这句话的另一层意思是——刚才那一幕的一切，她都已然知晓。
t_en: However, what this does mean... Is that everything that happened here... she knew it was coming.

t_ch: 我、我不能冒这个险，万一她真的背叛了我…我一个普通人在眷属面前，不就像蝼蚁一样脆弱吗？我可不能死，我不能死……
t_en: I... I cannot take this risk. What if she really betrayed me... I, a normal man... am I not as frail as an ant before the Titankin? I cannot die. I cannot die...

t_ch: 和敌国的女子相恋，背弃一切私奔，这在我读过的故事里很浪漫，所以我才迷了心窍……
t_en: To fall in love with a woman from a rival city and abandon everything as I flee. This is the kind of romance I read in my stories, and that's how I became obsessed...

t_ch: 帕里斯，快做出决定吧…守卫马上就要来了，我可不能帮一个想要潜逃的士兵洗脱罪名。
t_en: Paris. Make a decision quickly... The guards are coming. I cannot help clear the name of a soldier who attempts desertion.

t_ch: 谢谢你，俄诺涅，谢谢你救我……
t_en: Thank you, Oenone. Thank you for saving me...

t_ch: 听，快听。
t_en: Listen, listen now!

t_ch: 听，快听，灵魂传来了你熟悉的声音。
t_en: Listen, quick, listen. There is a familiar voice in your soul.

t_ch: 是纷争…纷争已经来到，神赐的金血在翻涌，命定的欲望在沸腾……
t_en: It is strife... Strife has come. The golden blood you were gifted begins to stew, and the fated desire boils up within you...

t_ch: 你要应这灵魂的呼声，投身纷争？还是握着手中的金丝，向远方遁逃？
t_en: Will you respond to the call of your soul and throw your body into the strife? Or will you hold on to this golden thread and escape into the distance ahead?

t_ch: 我不知道，这路的两端都是命运…一端是我的纷争，一端是他的浪漫……
t_en: I don't know. Both of these paths are my fate... One is my Strife, and the other is his Romance...

t_ch: 但本能的咆哮在城外的战场上是那么刺耳，于是，你还是选择射出了纷争的箭矢——
t_en: But the roar of your instincts deafens you on the battlefield, so you still opt to fire the Arrow of Strife that you wield...

t_ch: 第一个猎物，是你的同乡。他在军队的最前排，向你行起悬锋的礼仪，却被你贯穿了心脏。
t_en: Your first prey is your very own kin. On the Kremnos army's frontline, he gives you a salute, yet you shoot through his heart.

t_ch: 第二个猎物，是你的胞亲。他在攀爬城墙，想要在幕匿中潜入市集饱饮鲜血，却被你击碎了头颅。
t_en: Your second prey is your own family. He climbs the walls under the cover of night to feast on the dead, so you take your aim and shatter his head.

t_ch: 第三个、第四个、第五个…你不知疲倦地残害着你的同乡，却没有一箭射向奥赫玛人。
t_en: Number three, number four, number five... You ceaselessly hurt your own kin without stopping for rest. Yet your bow never aimed at any citizen of Okhema.

t_ch: 是那纤细的金丝还牵引着我的手，不令我的箭镞刺破那薄弱的浪漫——
t_en: It is the thin golden thread that still guides my hand. It will not allow my arrowheads to pierce that fragile romance...

t_ch: 你等了很久很久，甚至为奥赫玛的戍卫队击退了一波凶险的敌潮，但你没有等来与你远行的旅伴。
t_en: You wait and wait, and help the Garrison of Okhema fight off a whole wave of assault. But the man you would travel with never comes.

t_ch: 你想重回城邦，却又被城前的戍卫当作敌人阻拦。
t_en: You wish to return to the city, but the garrison guards greet you as an enemy.

t_ch: 你身上遍布圣城军与悬锋军的枪伤，你在这没有友亲的战场上寻找着失约的友亲——
t_en: As your body is covered in wounds dealt by both the army of the holy city and that of Kremnos, you search for the kin that missed his appointment in a battlefield bereft of kin...

t_ch: 帕…里斯……
t_en: Pa... ris...

t_ch: 那姑娘…天谴猎手一定是被她的同类唤起了纷争的本能，选择回到了悬锋城的军队中…背叛了我……
t_en: That girl... The Furiae Archer's battle instincts must have been awakened by her kin. She must have chosen to return to the forces of Castrum Kremnos... She has betrayed me...

t_ch: 没错，你是叛徒，你背叛了悬锋城，背叛了自己的同袍父兄，还想抛下这救赎你的圣城，你是不忠的叛徒。
t_en: That's right. You are a traitor. You betrayed Castrum Kremnos and your family. Then you set to abandon the holy city that saved you. You are exactly the traitor they made you.

t_ch: 放心吧，帕里斯。我会保护你的，让你远离那个灾星，帮你去往你理想中的地方。
t_en: Fear not, Paris. I will protect you. I will keep you away from that bringer of catastrophe and help you find the land of your dreams.

t_ch: 没错，你是灾星，你为奥赫玛带来了动荡，你让这对你有恩的城失守，你是纷争的灾星。
t_en: That's right. You bring calamity, and you've caused Okhema to fall. The city was kind to you, but you brought strife on them all.

t_ch: 让我断了这手上的金丝，莫让那叛徒寻到了我们的踪迹……
t_en: Let me cut this golden thread on my arm. I cannot have the traitor following us...

t_ch: 现在，那金丝已断了，你手中再也不会有首饰、厨具和针线，只剩长弓与利箭。
t_en: Now, the thread is cut. Your hands will never again hold silver trinkets, kitchenware, or tapestries. All that remains are arrows and weaponry.

t_ch: 而我也不必再做抉择，命运的路只剩一端——
t_en: Now, there are no more choices open for me. Only one path of fate remains...

t_ch: 前去纷争吧。
t_en: Go to the Strife.

t_ch: 在那之后，我做了一个漫长的梦。
t_en: From that moment on, I've been into a long dream.

t_ch: 梦中，我是一只瘦骨嶙峋的狮子，除了捕猎果腹外，没有思考其他事物的能力。
t_en: In my dream, I am a malnourished, starving lion. I can not think about anything but hunting for food.

t_ch: 于是我不停地捕猎，撕咬、杀戮。我第一次感受到生物的饥饿，所以我吞下了银饰、炉灶、没绣完的金毯……
t_en: So, I hunt, devour, and kill endlessly. I feel the biological sensation of hunger for the first time, so I swallow silver jewelry, cooking stoves, unfinished tapestries...

t_ch: ——直到我又看到了这枚绑着断线的指环，才在黑潮下的荒原中醒来。
t_en: ...Until I see the ring with the broken thread around it once more, and I come to my senses in the wastelands under the black tide.

t_ch: 戴着指环的男人躺在地上，我的箭让塞纳托斯的手掌捧走了他的灵魂。而与他同行的女子抛下了他，装饰花朵的马车头也不回地逃离了他的尸骸。
t_en: The man wearing the ring lies on the ground as my arrows guide the hand of Thanatos to claim his soul. The woman who travelled with him abandoned him. Their extravagant carriage, decked with flowers, sped away from his corpse without ever turning back.

t_ch: 到了现在，我也不明白，他说的「浪漫」到底是什么模样。
t_en: To this day, I still do not understand what this "romance" is that he spoke of.

t_ch: 神的行径有千模百样，神的行径不应众生期望。
t_en: The divine works in mysterious ways, and they care not for what a mortal man prays.

t_ch: 所料之事，却难圆满成就。不虞之事，却被神给予了道路。
t_en: An expectation is impossible for one to make real. When we know not where we're going, our path is revealed.

t_ch: 此事，便是如此结局。
t_en: This is how it ends.

t_ch: 随着记忆谢幕的余音散去，你们又回到了飘着硝烟的悬锋城。
t_en: As the lingering echoes of the memory fade, you return to the smoke-filled streets of Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 没有找到阿卡迪亚，也再也寻不回帕里斯，而没有名字的眷属还在你们面前呢喃。
t_en: You've never found Arcadia, and Paris will never return. Yet the nameless Titankin still lies before you, murmuring.

t_ch: 这个故事…好像和一开始想象的不一样呢……
t_en: This story... It ended differently to how I imagined...

t_ch: 帕…里斯……
t_en: Pa... ris...

t_ch: 泰坦的眷属的言语逐渐微弱，你将耳朵凑近，希望听到她到底想说什么——
t_en: As the Titankin's speech grows weaker, you bring your ear closer, hoping to hear her final words...

t_ch: 阿卡迪亚…阿卡迪亚……
t_en: Arcadia... Arcadia...

t_ch: 到底…在哪里呢……
t_en: Where... Could it be...

t_ch: 纷争的眷属不再发出任何言语，她一动不动，仿佛成了真的雕像。
t_en: The Titankin of Strife utters nothing more. She falls completely still, as if she had become a real statue.

t_ch: 伙伴，她的记忆…消散了……
t_en: Partner... her memory... It's disappeared...

t_ch: 失去生命的眷属尸首与碎石无异。你知道过不了多久，悬锋城带着血腥味的风会卷起沙砾，将这块巨石磨蚀成尘，飘往远方。
t_en: Having lost her life, the Titankin's body is no different from a pile of rubble. You know that before long, the blood-stained winds of Castrum Kremnos will whip up the surrounding gravel and reduce it to dust, carrying it far into the distance.

t_ch: 尘土飘向的地方会是真正的阿卡迪亚吗？{RUBY_B#理性}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}知晓那个地方吗？{RUBY_B#浪漫}墨涅塔{RUBY_E#}会在那里等候它吗？
t_en: Perhaps the land that this dust will be carried to is the true Arcadia? Does {RUBY_B#Reason}Cerces{RUBY_E#} know that place? Will {RUBY_B#Romance}Mnestia{RUBY_E#} await them there?

t_ch: 你不知道，你耳边只有{RUBY_B#纷争}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的战鼓回荡。
t_en: You do not know. All that you can hear is the echoing war drums of {RUBY_B#Strife}Nikador{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅女士，请稍等一会儿，那位异乡的英雄已经出发了……
t_en: Lady Aglaea, please wait a moment. The hero from afar has already set forth...

t_ch: 啊，是已经回来了！英雄，您找回那宝物了吗？
t_en: Ah, they have returned already! Hero, did you recover the treasure?

t_ch: 是的。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 这件宝物真的很沉重。
t_en: This treasure truly is precious.

t_ch: 希望阿格莱雅能保管好它…
t_en: I hope you will keep it safe, Lady Aglaea...

t_ch: 辛苦了，贵客，真没想到这份委托会劳烦到你。
t_en: Thank you for your hard work, dear guest. I had never expected that this task would have fallen to you.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅从你的手中接过献礼，她双目微阖，感受着纺锤上每根金丝的触感……
t_en: Aglaea takes the offering from your hand and closes her eyes as she feel across every golden thread on the spindle.

t_ch: 伊阿宋队长，很遗憾。
t_en: Captain Iason, I have unfortunate news.

t_ch: 怎、怎么了？
t_en: Wh—What is it?

t_ch: 观察贵客方才的神色，它应当是承载了什么特殊的意义，或许在某些人眼中是十分贵重的宝物。
t_en: Seeing the look in our dear guest's eyes just now, I fear this object may carry some special meaning. It could be that this object was considered extremely precious to someone before.

t_ch: 但若让我以「理性」来评判，它既不神圣，也不贵重——
t_en: However, if you are to judge it through the eyes of Reason, it has neither divinity nor value...

t_ch: 只是一捆再平凡不过的，染了色的细线。
t_en: It is no more than a simple and unremarkable dyed thread.

t_ch: 它在他人耳边缄默，它在自己耳边喧哗。
t_en: Though it speaks not to another one's ear, I can hear it loud and clear.

t_ch: 它只在看到它的人眼中显现，只在相信它的人眼中璀璨。
t_en: Beauty is in the beholder's eyes, and only those that believe it will see it shine.

t_ch: ——黄金茧中的浪漫丝，便是如此缥缈物。
t_en: ...The threads of romance in the golden cocoon, a mystery known to only those attuned.

t_ch: 嗡嗡…嗡嗡……
t_en: Buzz... Buzz...

t_ch: 这只若虫在悬锋城中格外醒目。你知道，弥漫血腥味的废墟并不是浪漫眷属乐意造访的地方。
t_en: A nymph is an unfamiliar sight to the streets of Castrum Kremnos. As you know, ruins stained with the stench of blood are not a place that the Titankin of Romance would happily visit.

t_ch: 嗡嗡——我是「浪漫」的使者，顺应「浪漫」的渴望而来，代表「浪漫」予以信徒回应。
t_en: Buzz... I am an envoy of Romance. I respond to those that long for Romance as its disciple.

t_ch: 陌生人，你知道是谁在此处呼唤墨涅塔吗？
t_en: Stranger, do you know who it was that summoned Mnestia to this place?

t_ch: 那个人已经…
t_en: They are already...

t_ch: 你来的太迟了…
t_en: You're too late...

t_ch: 嗡…我明白了…真遗憾，希望塞纳托斯能早日把她送回我们的茧中。
t_en: Buzz... I understand... What a terrible shame. I hope that Thanatos will allow her to join our cocoon soon.

t_ch: 谢谢你，陌生人，那我就先回茧了！
t_en: Thank you, stranger. I shall return to the cocoon!

t_ch: 先别走…
t_en: Don't go...

t_ch: 我有问题想问你…
t_en: Can I ask a question...?

t_ch: 嗡？怎么了？
t_en: Buzz? What's wrong?

t_ch: 阿卡迪亚在什么地方？
t_en: Where is Arcadia?

t_ch: 阿卡迪亚——
t_en: Arcadia...

t_ch: 那是什么东西？
t_en: What's that?

t_ch: （那个是…尼卡多利的眷属？怎么会出现在这里？）
t_en: (That is... the Titankin of Nikador? What are they doing here?)

t_ch: 好像有股香味…不对，是陌生记忆的味道，从那里传过来的！
t_en: I can smell a sweet fragrance... No, this is the fragrance of an unknown memory! It's coming from over there!

t_ch: 这里的店主每天都会遇到各种各样的客人，也算是见多识广，就问问他们吧。
t_en: The store owners here meet all kinds of people every day. They'll be the most knowledgeable about the area. We should ask some of them.

t_ch: 这里就是雕像小姐记忆中的过去了，但她在哪里呢？
t_en: This is the past in the statue lady's memory... But where is she?

t_ch: 唔，果然打不开，不过他们说的神性似乎在哪里听过……
t_en: Agh, we really can't open it. Although, I feel like I've heard about that deity they mentioned somewhere before...

t_ch: 想起来了，这些雕像就储存着尼卡多利的神性！不过我们也不能把它们在这里砸碎吧？
t_en: Come to think of it, these statues store the divinity of Nikador! We can't just smash them here and now though, can we?

t_ch: 可以靠欧洛尼斯的力量。
t_en: We can use Oronyx's power.

t_ch: 我们得再回到另一个时空。
t_en: We need to return to the other spacetime.

t_ch: 这玩意儿和之前的解密一样吧…
t_en: This is the same puzzle as before, surely...

t_ch: 是哦，那找找附近有没有「欧洛尼斯奇迹」的秘典吧！
t_en: You're right. Let's look around to see if there is another secret edition of "Oronyx's Miracle"!

t_ch: 哇，他居然能抬动雕像小姐，力气真大…快跟上，要是让他们遇到卫兵就麻烦了！
t_en: Woah, he actually managed to lift the statue lady! He's so strong... We have to catch up with him. If they run into the guards, then we're in big trouble!

t_ch: 不好了！献给尼卡多利的祭品逃跑了！
t_en: We've got big trouble! The sacrifices to Nikador have escaped!

t_ch: 怎么可能？囚房明明没法从内部打开…那个眷属应该已经不能动了，他们肯定走不远！
t_en: How is that possible? The prison gates cannot be opened from inside... That Titankin can't walk anymore, so they can't have gone far!

t_ch: 帕里斯应该已经带着雕像小姐已经进城了，她会在哪里接受治疗呢……
t_en: Paris must have already taken the statue lady into the city. Where will she be able to receive treatment, though?

t_ch: 赞美法吉娜！赞美云石天宫！
t_en: Praise Phagousa! Praise Marmoreal Palace!

t_ch: 哇，这里是正在开派对吗？好热闹！
t_en: Wow, are they having a party? This place is so busy!

t_ch: 嗯？她怎么还在城里，难道是迷路了？
t_en: Hm? Why is she still in the city? Has she gotten lost?

t_ch: 还有戍卫长和俄诺涅…好像不太对劲！
t_en: The captain and Oenone are there, too... Something isn't right!

t_ch: 雕像小姐，你的记忆，我们会记住的……
t_en: Statue lady... Your memory... We will never forget it...

t_ch: 她会在哪里呢…如果是人家，或许会找个视野开阔的地方吧……
t_en: Where could she be? If I were her, I might look for a place with open views...

t_ch: 这段回忆的味道好奇怪…嗯？帕里斯在那儿干什么呢？
t_en: This memory tastes weird... Hm? What is Paris doing there?

t_ch: 这里的通路被挡住了…不知道有没有其他泰坦的力量能帮一把「手」。
t_en: The passage here has been obstructed... I wonder if any other Titan's strength could lend a helping "hand."

t_ch: 这台装置好像缺了什么东西，看来还得靠扎格列斯来帮忙了……
t_en: There seems to be something missing from this device. It looks like I'll need to find Zagreus...

t_ch: 开场的记忆
t_en: Opening Memory

t_ch: 旧日的悬锋城，一场失败的战争后……
t_en: The former Castrum Kremnos, after a defeat in battle...

t_ch: 在记忆的更深处，传来了枷锁的碰撞声……
t_en: From the depths of the memory comes the clash of shackles...

t_ch: 第一场的记忆
t_en: The First Memory

t_ch: 旧日的奥赫玛城门，一场胜利的战争后……
t_en: The former Okhema city gate, after a victorious war...

t_ch: 在记忆的更深处，传来了市集的议论声……
t_en: From the depths of the memory comes the vibrant bustle of the marketplace...

t_ch: 第二场的记忆
t_en: The Second Memory

t_ch: 旧日的奥赫玛城中，一位战士在岁月的流淌中迷惘着……
t_en: Within the old Okhema, a soldier is lost in the flow of time...

t_ch: 在记忆的更深处，传来了派对的喧闹声……
t_en: From the depths of the memory emerge the sounds of a lively party...

t_ch: 第三场的记忆
t_en: The Third Memory

t_ch: 许久后的奥赫玛城中，无休止的纷争再度苏醒……
t_en: A long time later, within the city of Okhema, the echoes of endless strife resurface once more...

t_ch: 在记忆的更深处，传来伴随愁绪的哀叹……
t_en: From the depths of the memory arises a sorrow lament...

t_ch: 退场的记忆
t_en: Receding Memory

t_ch: 在奥赫玛的城外，战士徘徊在注定的疯狂中……
t_en: On the outskirts of Okhema, the soldiers wander in doomed madness...

t_ch: 戍卫长倒伏在鲜血中，充斥嗔怨的胸口停止了起伏……
t_en: The captain collapses in a pool of fresh blood. His chest, once heaving with rage, now still...

t_ch: （要让这么大的东西动起来，或许只能靠「欧洛尼斯祷言了」……）
t_en: (Maybe the only way to get something this big to move is to use the "Oronyx's Prayer"...)

t_ch: 咦？这里的剑已经归位了…估计是那两个笨家伙没注意吧。
t_en: Huh? The sword here is back in its place... I'm guessing those two idiots didn't even notice it.

t_ch: 阿…阿卡迪亚…帕里斯……
t_en: Ar... Arcadia... Paris...

t_ch: 泰坦眷属口中吐出破碎的词语，夹杂着残存不多的生气。
t_en: The Titankin can only muster fractions of words, carried on the little life force she has left.

t_ch: 伙伴，快抓紧时间吧！
t_en: Friend! We have to be quick!

t_ch: 阿…亚…斯……
t_en: Ar... ca... Paris...

t_ch: 泰坦眷属的话语已微弱到无法听清，看来你们真的要快些了。
t_en: The Titankin's words have already grown too faint to discern. Looks like you really need to get moving.

t_ch: 就靠你了，好用…不，万能的大恩人！
t_en: I'm counting on you, my handy... I mean, my all-powerful savior!

t_ch: 呃，这是什么东西挡着入口……
t_en: Ugh, what's obstructing the entrance...

t_ch: 这尊纷争眷属的雕像死死压住牢房的门，
t_en: A statue of the Strife Titankin presses against the cell door...

t_ch: 能挪动吗？
t_en: Is it possible to move it?

t_ch: 开拓力转动！你运转周天，双臂发力，气动山河——
t_en: Unleash the spiraling Trailblaze Power! You ignite the energy within, channeling it through your arms, gathering strength enough to move mountains and rivers...

t_ch: 它纹丝不动，看来你这块儿还得接着练练。
t_en: It hasn't budged. It seems like you will need more practice.

t_ch: 砸碎它！
t_en: Smash it!

t_ch: 一百万匹开拓力！你的球棒狠狠砸在雕像的头上——
t_en: Unleash a million horsepower worth of Trailblaze Power! You swing your ball bat and strike the statue's head with all your might —

t_ch: 它坚不可摧，甚至韧性条都没动。
t_en: It is indestructible. Not even a dent appears on its Toughness bar.
